reviews.review_description: Half my screen has this strange blackness over half of the left side I have uninstllaed and remove and reinstalled it 3 times and it's still there, nothing I do removes it sadly, well gutted
In the game has this black screen covered half the vision.

What a refreshing surprise this game was. A simple concept that just made me smile and sometimes that's all you need from video games!

Check out my first impressions gameplay video over on my YouTube!

😁✌️☮️❤️

reviews.review_title: Devs: Rotate on alt stick for seated?
reviews.review_description:
Hey devs, really great work. I love the game, but it's best played on a swivel chair. Would love to be able to play just seated easily, so any chance of adding a turn/rotate world option on non-plane-hand stick? Other similar games have this, works well. Just s suggestion, but a valid one I think. Cheers!
Update: Thanks for the response, was suggesting as an option to turn on or off but I get the initial design choice and limited resources etc. Cheers!

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for playing the game. We had this discussion internally early in development and decided not to add the snap turns since it breaks flow. We think that Volara should be  experienced with real turning. We are a small team and successfully implementing a feature like that would’ve eaten development time from other stuff that we deemed more valuable for players. We might’ve been wrong but we decided it always thinking in what would benefit most players.

reviews.review_description: I absolutely loved playing this game. I could only play it when I was connected to my PC before.  Now that it's standalone it's so much better as I can throw on my headset at anytime and jump in for a quick session. This game is fun relaxing and has elements that will frustrate you in a good way.  I highly recommend Volara.  Great job flamingo studios.

so let me start off this review by saying this is known as Kid Pilot on Steam, and has been renamed to Volara for this quest version. Kid Pilot is well-rated and for good reason!

this is a very beautiful game with amazing environments and great music. the premise of the game is exactly what the store page says: just move your hand to control the plane! but, it's all just so pretty! it has a gorgeous artstyle. even just the tutorial level is beautiful - you fly through rings that make music, and the sky passes you by and just.. it's just so pretty. have i mentioned how pretty it is yet?

you may be controlling a toy plane, but you yourself also feel like you're soaring through the sky. it's an amazing feeling, but beware if you're sensitive to motion sickness! you need VR legs for this one.

you have to play this either standing up or seated on a chair that can spin around freely. there is no smooth/snap turn. but in my opinion, that's not a bad thing, it makes it all the more immersive. but be aware of this in case it's an issue for you.

i got this game with the 90% off discount code that's currently out there but don't let the cheap price fool you. this is a GREAT game and for that 90% off it's an absolute steal. this is worth it even at full price!

reviews.review_title: Great game for a Great price!!
reviews.review_description: I was playing it with an early access, and i like how every world has a different objective and style, composed with a unique soundtrack that fits with the level mood. The difficulty grows in every level so be prepared for the last one. Enjoy it!

reviews.review_title: Very fun--needs option for SEATED play
reviews.review_description:
I bought this game on a whim and ended up really enjoying it! It has the look and charm of an N64 title (which is a compliment). The gameplay is straightforward, and I appreciate that the player controls the speed. The intuitive mechanic of controlling the plane with your hand is wonderful.

However, my only complaint is that the game currently lacks a mode for seated play, which I consider a significant issue. For instance, while "flying," there's no way to reorient yourself. It is extremely difficult to turn around and get reoriented when you pass by something, as the game lacks any "turn" input.

For example, when flying in a straight line and needing to turn around and backtrack, you can turn your hand (holding the plane) around backwards and move backwards, but you'll be facing the wrong direction. Trying to do a wide, gradual, forward-facing turn is neither easy nor viable. While playing seated, I found myself repeatedly twisting my body to face backward to reorient my plane. The developers should address this issue quickly, as it will likely frustrate casual gamers.

That aside, I've thoroughly enjoyed the game and plan on completing all the missions. PLEASE, DEVS—provide a seated option!

developer_response.body:
Hello, and thanks for your review. We considered adding that feature, but we think it goes against the way the game should be played. After many testing, we decided the best way to play the game is in a spinning chair. Try it! Volara is all about flow, once you try it in a spinning chair you'll see what we are talking about.

Try it and if you agree, give us 5 stars! :)

reviews.review_description: The UI is confusing and pressing the menu button has no effect. Somehow everything is in Japanese, which I later found that there's an option at the title screen that can only be reached if I crouch. Graphics is underwhelming, with barely any 3D effect (due to everything being far away). Gameplay is pretty boring, at least for the first level, and later levels are unreachable yet (and I lose interest).

developer_response.body:
The first level “Girafloat” is meant to be very simple to help people get a hold of flying before the challenge begins. We urge you to try and get to Level 2 “Topo Tron”. We think you will like it then. 

It’s hard to calibrate the difficulty progression in VR since this game sometimes is people’s first VR game ever. So for veterans it might start a bit slow. But trust us, it ramps up!

Also just try and be more intrepid with your flying.  When we were testing the game sometimes we would just fly around trying to go trough tight gaps, or speed run the levels. To us that is still a lot of fun. Like those drone videos that go trough the structure of a building

reviews.review_description: For such simple graphics what are there so much jagged edges...needs serious work...its fun...but an insult to the power of the quest 3...

reviews.review_title: Challenging and great for begin in VR
reviews.review_description:
GAMEPLAY: The first Level of toy land was so easy, i thought was the tutorial, but after second level, the difficulty increase and the game turns interesting. I like how every level had different ways to pass, and the final boss was so scary haha.  Controllers are easy.

CONTENT: Each world has different style, music and goal to pass, i like that changes to avoid monotony. You need to find how unlock extra skins of the planes, i unlock 2 surprisly and that was a nice detail.

CONCLUSION: Im starting to play VR games and this is a very good start, no motion sickness and lightly movements to play. If you like Rare and nintendo games of 2000s and 90s, you will love the nostalgic feeling.

reviews.review_title: Good fun at first but needs an update
reviews.review_description:
UPDATE 20.1.25 
Wow, you fixed the left handed glitch extremely quickly. I had another go for over twenty minutes and still can’t get passed the race. It seemed even faster this time round too. The same with topo tron, that’s even tougher to complete from the start. 

I have revised my review to three stars for the quick fix which seems fair. This is not a game for me at the moment, as unfortunately I needed a slightly easier game. I’m glad I got it in the sale. I wish you well though and look forward to more updates in the future as mentioned on my initial review below. 

Oh and not sure why but on my first start I was messing about shooting and did something shoot me or crash into me. I’m not sure but my plane exploded and I had to start again. It was fine on the second attempt though, so maybe just a one off.

————————— 
UPDATE 16.1.25. 
Thanks for the quick reply and tips. I tried taking the batteries out pre game as you suggested and couldn’t do anything. My plane disappeared. I’d suggest you trying to setup left handed and go from there. It can’t just be my version of the game that has this issue surely. The giraffe level is fine, inbetween levels are fine but as soon as you go into one of the other unlocked ones it seems broken. Also tried the other tip of not going through the red rings. That is pretty much almost impossible too. I managed to get to almost the end after a few tries, but it’s ridiculously difficult. 

Hopefully there is a fix soon, as at the moment it’s unplayable past the first boss, for me at least. Down to two stars at the moment I’m afraid. 

————————— 
ORIGINAL REVIEW 12.1.25. 
I have played just over an hour in total. After 22 minutes I did feel a little nauseous. I was also disappointed it took two and a half minutes to even start the game. Although it did say it was a one off and subsequent starts were a lot quicker. The only other game of the hundreds I’ve played that took longer was assassins creed nexus. The first levels took me almost 20 minutes, admittedly that included some exploring and just messing about and for the sake of it. I must say, it was a really fun and relaxed experience though. The game looks good too. Overall a pretty positive first impression. 

On my second attempt I tried another twenty something minutes. I completed the chilled out first Giraffe levels again and it was still quite fun. After that it seemed the only option was a boss battle. That was frustrating as it really wasn’t clear what to do and I was forced to play right handed, even though previously everything was done with my left. I was tempted to refund at this point. 

Third time lucky they say and it was.  I played for under twenty minutes and finished the first boss despite still being forced to play right handed. I’m not sure how I did it though, but it was relief to finish it. I then started the next race, when again I was auto switched to using my right hand. I am hopeful this will be fixed, as I am too late to refund now. It started off OK. Then, it did get quite fast though and I struggled somewhat and had to stop. 

Overall, i am rating it a somewhat generous 4 stars. It’s frustrating having to play right handed and I do think there should be some options for comfort and also speed and difficulty too. Maybe. Beginner, novice and advanced. If you could easily switch between them that would be goodr too. 

I did feel a bit nauseous on the high speed race and have yet to complete it, as what I thought were lights was actually fire. I haven’t had another go since. 

I look forward to some big fixes and hopefully anti nausea settings too.

I got an early access to this game and fell in love with it. Is a lot of fun to fly in VR like this. Super intuitive and not dizzying at all. This could be played by anyone. I would even recommend it as a first VR game.

The last boss was HARD! But very satisfying once I bested it. The game has many challenges and secret airplane skins.

Flying around in the hub “Volara Radio” was very entertaining. I spent hours playing hoops.

reviews.review_title: Good for introducing you in VR Games
reviews.review_description: First of all, I love the music of every level!, it gave me the different feeling vibes of every situation and you enjoy it while playing. The world designs reminds me the n64 adventure games style, really good touch.  Im lefty  and it works, the controller was very easy to use.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing :) and for your review we grew up playing nintendo 64.

reviews.review_description: The game is a brilliant idea. But I found bugs from the beginning .the  devs are kindly trying to fix

developer_response.body: Hello. Sorry you had this issue. It seems like a bug with new meta update V72. Other games have been experiencing this. Please email us to pilotone@flamingosimulation.com to let us know some details of this error and see if we can work together to fix it or find a way we can make it up to you.

reviews.review_description: I really enjoy the flow of flying in the immersive worlds of Volara. There are clever puzzles to solve and the music is great. The best experience is to sit on a wheeled office chair so you have 360 movement.

Volara is an amazing experience that is perfect for all ages and great as an introduction to vr. I played in on steam. But now that it is stand alone it is even better. I wish that it was longer, definitely worth it.

Edit: i could change the language now and its even in german (and many other languages). Thank you. Very great & chill game. 5 Stars!

Game seems to be fun. But at the beginning the game is asking for language. I wanted to skip through the language options, but it startet in korean. And there are no options in the game to switch back to english. Please fix that. Than i give 5 stars. I dont understand anything in the game.

developer_response.body:
You can change the language at any time. When you start the game in your case it should appear < KOR >. Hit those arrows with the glowing ball until you see ENG.  Do not grab the airplane until you’ve chosen the language or else the game will start in the selected language. 

Also if you are already playing, and if you are a the tower (volara radio), go to the main menu by pausing the game with A and then select return to main menu with B. You’ll be teleported to the main menu where you can select language.

Hope this helps! If you have any problems you can write to us to pilotone@flamingosimulation.com
And if it works, please do give us 5 stars✈️

reviews.review_title: Unique and fun game with bad graphics
reviews.review_description: The idea of flying with plane held in player's hand is brilliant. It works superb. Various ideas for levels keep the game interesting. Unfortunately graphics are bad - very basic, low resolution with some framerate drops. Games on quest 3 shouldn't look and perform like that :(

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing. We decided to super optimize the game at a cost of graphics in order to have Quest 2 players onboard. Moreover, our game has many optimization challenges, like moving freely at fast speeds on an environment with 360 degrees of freedom. At some point we thought it would be imposible to port. We invite you to play the PCVR version of the game. Write to us to pilotone@flamingosimulation.com and we will give you a key so you can enjoy the game in all its graphic’s glory.

reviews.review_description: I got this at a discount and bought it before I watched anything about it. After watching the video on what the game was I thought, " Not another plane game where you control the aircraft with the controllers". Since I didn't pay much I wasn't bothering to get a refund. I am so glad I played it. I love that you hold the airplane and move it around to get the objectives. It reminds me of being a child again and pretending it is a real plane and flying around the house. This game is perfect for VR.
